questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If it rains, the match is cancelled. The match was not cancelled. Therefore,
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Modus tollens: deny the consequent to deny the antecedent.

**Working.** From 'rain → cancelled' and 'not cancelled', conclude 'not rain'.

**Answer.** It did not rain.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Using A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, the sum of the letter values of the word 'ORE' is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Replace each letter with its position value and add.

**Working.** O$=15$, R$=18$, E$=5$; sum $=38$.

**Answer.** $38$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A clock loses $5$ minutes every hour. After $12$ hours of running, it is _____ minutes behind the correct time.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total loss $=$ rate of loss $\times$ time.

**Working.** $5\,\text{min/h}\times 12\,\text{h}=60$ minutes.

**Answer.** $60$ minutes.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Identify the part that contains an error: 'One of the pumps (A) / are not working (B) / properly in (C) / the lower level. (D)'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The phrase 'one of the + plural noun' takes a singular verb because the true subject is 'one'.

**Working.** The subject is 'one', so the verb should be 'is not working', not 'are not working'. The error lies in part B.

**Answer.** B.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A person faces north, turns $90^\circ$ clockwise, then turns $180^\circ$. Which direction is the person now facing?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Track each turn from the current facing.

**Working.** North → ($90^\circ$ CW) East → ($180^\circ$) West.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: The progressive loss of porosity in a sandstone with burial is mainly due to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mechanical compaction and cementation reduce porosity during diagenesis.

**Working.**
Compaction & cementation.

**Answer.** Compaction and cementation

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $55^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=55^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $55^\circ$

questions.subject: Geophysics
questions.stem: The total geomagnetic field is $50000\,$nT with inclination $60^\circ$. The horizontal component is _____ nT.

questions.subject: Mathematics & Geostatistics
questions.stem: The trapezoidal rule approximates the area under a curve using
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trapezoidal rule joins successive points by straight lines.

**Working.**
Straight-line segments.

**Answer.** Straight-line segments

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: On an equal-area (Schmidt) stereonet, a plane is represented as a

figure.caption: A dipping plane as a great circle

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A plane plots as a great circle; its pole plots as a point.

**Working.**
Great circle.

**Answer.** Great circle
